title: astrantia major subsp. elatior
intro: Astrantia major subsp. elatior, also known as the Great Masterwort, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its roots and rhizomes. The health benefits of Astrantia major subsp. elatior include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
